title: Complete HTML template rewrite for compact layout
description: Restructure the HTML template (lines 1302-1360 in panel/index.js) to support the compact design system. Key changes: (1) merge results-meta into search toolbar row, (2) replace empty state with single-line text placeholder, (3) reduce skeleton loading to 2 compact items, (4) restructure settings modal shell into settings page container, (5) ensure all 17 $ element binding IDs remain present. The template must produce a layout where search toolbar is one 24px row, results are a flat list, and component bar is a compact horizontal strip.

criteria: All 17 $ element binding IDs present in template: btn, refresh-btn, com_name, list, list-items, list-loading, list-empty, result-count, main, tip, coms, coms-wrap, settings-btn, settings-modal, settings-content, close-settings-btn, and .settings-dialog CSS selector
Results-meta (#result-count) moved inline into search toolbar row (same row as input + buttons)
Empty state (#list-empty) is a single <div> with muted text, no icon, no large block
Loading state (#list-loading) contains max 2 skeleton items at compact size
Settings shell (#settings-modal) restructured as full-panel container (not overlay), with #close-settings-btn as back arrow, #settings-content as scrollable body
Main container (#main) uses flex column layout with no gap property
Template has no elements with hardcoded 44px sizes or large padding inline styles
convergence.verification: Parse the template HTML string and verify all 17 $ binding IDs exist. Open panel in Cocos Creator and verify layout matches design system mockup.
convergence.definition_of_done: The HTML template produces the correct DOM structure for the compact IDE layout, with all element bindings preserved for JS functionality.

changes: Restructure search row: single .toolbar div containing input#com_name, span#result-count (inline), ui-button#btn, ui-button#refresh-btn, ui-button#settings-btn

Replace large empty state block with compact:
${t('PANEL.SEARCH_PLACEHOLDER')}
Reduce skeleton loading items from 6 to 2, remove skeleton-grid class
Convert settings-modal from overlay structure to page structure: remove overlay div, make settings-modal a direct child of container with hidden-by-default display
Change close-settings-btn from X icon to back arrow icon
Remove results-meta wrapper div (count is now inline in toolbar)
Ensure #main wraps the main content view (toolbar + list + component bar) for show/hide toggle with settings page
Add ARIA attributes: role=toolbar on search row, role=listbox on list-items, role=option on each result

actions: Create single .toolbar div wrapping: input#com_name, span#result-count (after input, inline), ui-button#btn (add), ui-button#refresh-btn, ui-button#settings-btn
Use svgIcon('plus', {size:'sm'}) for add button, svgIcon('refreshCw', {size:'sm'}) for refresh, svgIcon('settings', {size:'sm'}) for settings
Replace empty state content: single div with muted text t('PANEL.SEARCH_PLACEHOLDER'), remove SVG icon block
Reduce skeleton items from 6 to 2, use compact skeleton class
Ensure #list-loading and #list-empty remain as separate togglable containers inside #list

implementation.description: Convert settings modal to settings page
actions: Make #settings-modal a sibling of #main (both direct children of .container)
Add .settings-header row with back button (#close-settings-btn with svgIcon('arrowLeft')) and title
Settings page hidden by default (display:none), shown by adding .active class and hiding #main
Cross-check all 17 $ map entries against template IDs
Verify .settings-dialog CSS class exists on appropriate container (used as $ selector)
Test that this.$btn, this.$refreshBtn, this.$eb, etc. all resolve correctly

reference.examples: The $ map uses {key: '#id'} format. The key becomes this.$key. All IDs must match exactly.

rationale.chosen_approach: Restructure template to eliminate wasted vertical space by merging rows and simplifying states, while preserving all element binding IDs required by the JS layer.
alternatives_considered: Keep existing template structure and only modify CSS (insufficient - HTML structure creates unnecessary wrapper divs)
Completely rewrite template from scratch (risky - may miss subtle element bindings)
rationale.tradeoffs: Template diff is significant; careful verification of all bindings required

risks.description: Missing a $ element binding ID would break JS functionality silently
risks.mitigation: Automated check: grep all $ keys and verify each ID exists in template
risks.fallback: Keep a pre-refactor backup of the template string for comparison

manual_checks: All 17 $ element bindings resolve (no null references in ready())
Settings page toggles correctly (main hidden, settings shown)
Empty state shows single-line muted text
Search toolbar shows input + count + 3 icon buttons in one row
success_metrics: 17/17 $ element bindings verified present in template
Search toolbar fits in single 24px row
Zero instances of skeleton-grid with 6 items

commit.message_template: refactor(panel-html): restructure template for compact IDE layout
